title: Bridge Essay: Neural Correlates of Metacognition Impairment in Opioid Addiction.
concept: Metacognition as the 'diagnostic' capacity — science of knowing what you know, parallel to esoteric self-examination.

body.text: This bridge paper connects the esoteric/philosophical concept of **Dashboard Diagnosis** with mechanistic science from PubMed/MEDLINE.
**Bridge rationale:** Metacognition as the 'diagnostic' capacity — science of knowing what you know, parallel to esoteric self-examination.

**Neural Correlates of Metacognition Impairment in Opioid Addiction.** - Biological psychiatry. Cognitive neuroscience and neuroimaging (2024). DOI: 10.1016/j.bpsc.2024.07.014. PMID: 39059467. BACKGROUND: Individuals with substance use disorder show impaired self-awareness of ongoing behavior. This deficit suggests problems with metacognition, which has been operationalized in the cognitive neuroscience literature as the ability to monitor and evaluate the success of one's own cognition and behavior. However, the neural mechanisms of metacognition have not been characterized in a population with drug addiction. Individuals with substance use disorder show impaired self-awareness of on
